data.title: Crypto News Today: Bitcoin Crashes 31% From Its High to $87K as $1 Trillion Is Wiped Out; Ethereum Slides 44% to $2.8K
data.description: The cryptocurrency market is reeling as Bitcoin fell to around $87,300, its lowest level in seven months, and Ethereum slipped to about $2,810, dragging more than $1 trillion in market value out of the digital-asset world. The correction is intensifying amid macro-uncertainty and fading institutional momentum.What to KnowBitcoin is trading near $87,300, a sharp fall from its October peak and now below its 2025 start level.Ethereum is trading around $2,810, having relinquished most of its earlier gains.The total crypto market cap has dropped from about $4.3 trillion at its October peak to roughly $3.2 trillion, indicating a loss of over $1 trillion.The U.S. economy added 119,000 jobs in September and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%, fueling market risk-off sentiment.Crypto markets are increasingly moving in line with macro assets, not acting as a safe haven.The Crash’s Contours: What’s Driving the Wipe-OutBitcoin’s drop below $90,000 and Ethereum’s slide below $2,900 signal the rally earlier this year has reversed. The market’s total capitalization peaked near $4.3 trillion on October 6 but now sits near $3.2 trillion, marking roughly $1 trillion in value runoff.The October 10 cascade—when more than $19 billion in leveraged crypto positions were liquidated—exposed structural fragilities. Forced selling, ETF outflows, and risk-off positioning are now converging to drive deeper corrections.“Investors are stabbing in the dark a bit — they haven’t got any direction on macro, so all they can see is what on-chain whales are doing and they’re getting quite worried about it,” said James Butterfill, head of research at CoinShares.Macro Backdrop: Jobs Data, Fed Expectations and Risk OffThe delayed U.S. jobs report revealed non-farm payrolls rose by 119,000 in September, exceeding forecasts of about 50,000, but the unemployment rate climbed to 4.4%. The mixed data signals labour-market softness despite continuing hiring.Markets interpreted the outcome as reducing the odds of an early rate cut by the Federal Reserve. That shift has weighed heavily on risk assets, including crypto, which now trades more like a correlated asset rather than an alternative hedge.Crypto Markets: Why the Damage Is so Broad-BasedCorrelation with equities and macro risk – Bitcoin and Ethereum now move in tandem with global risk sentiment.Leverage and ETF outflows – With active outflows from crypto ETFs and heavy liquidations, selling pressure is intensified.Technical breakdowns – Breach of key levels such as $90K for Bitcoin and ~$2,900 for Ethereum triggered algorithmic selling.Institutional pullback – Earlier conviction from institutions is fading as rate-cut expectations dim.Price Context: Where Things StandBitcoin (BTC): ~$87,300 — lowest in seven months, down sharply from its ~ $126,200 October high.Ethereum (ETH): ~$2,810 — losing virtually all earlier gains, well under prior resistance around $3,100–$3,200.What to Watch NextKey Levels to MonitorBitcoin: $85K – $88K as near support; below that, next major support ~$80K.Ethereum: ~$2,700–$2,800 as critical near-term support; upside key level ~$3,150–$3,200.Macro & Market TriggersFed policy signals and U.S. inflation/jobs data.Global trade risks, particularly U.S. tariff announcements.ETF flow data and crypto-specific leverage dynamics.Sentiment and Structural IndicatorsOn-chain metrics showing whale behaviour and accumulation vs. dumping.Liquidity flows in derivatives markets and ETF outflows.Risk-off behaviour in traditional assets as an early signal for crypto moves.OutlookWhile painful, the recent correction may be moving toward a consolidation phase rather than a collapse, according to some analysts.However, both Bitcoin and Ethereum will require a clear shift—either through resurgent institutional flows, macro stability or strong on-chain accumulation—to break the downtrend.Until then, expect crypto markets to continue reacting to global risk sentiment, rather than their past narrative of independent growth.

data.title: Crypto News: SEC Enforcement Actions Dropped 30% Under Paul Atkins, Cornerstone Report Shows
data.description: Enforcement activity at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has fallen sharply under Chair Paul Atkins, with total actions against public companies and their subsidiaries dropping about 30% in fiscal year 2025 compared with the final year of former Chair Gary Gensler, according to a new report from Cornerstone Research.The decline aligns with historical patterns seen whenever SEC leadership changes, but analysts note it also reflects the agency’s shift in regulatory philosophy — particularly around digital assets.A Sharp Drop in Enforcement CasesCornerstone’s report, released Wednesday, found:30% fewer enforcement actions under Chair Atkins in FY2025Decreases across categories including issuer reporting, accounting, disclosure failures, and corporate governanceMarked reduction in crypto-related actions compared with the Gensler eraAlthough the SEC dropped several high-profile crypto investigations after Gensler’s departure, Cornerstone noted that the report itself referenced only one case explicitly — the dismissal of the SEC’s lawsuit against Coinbase in February.The reduction comes after the agency spent 43 days operating with limited staff during the federal government shutdown, which slowed reviews, investigations, and litigation.Shift in SEC Priorities Under AtkinsCornerstone said the new numbers are “consistent with the stated priorities of the current SEC administration.”Chair Paul Atkins has signaled repeatedly that his top priority is building a “rational, coherent, and principled approach” to regulating digital assets. His stance contrasts with Gensler’s more aggressive interpretations of securities laws and extensive crypto enforcement posture.The SEC’s Division of Examinations released its FY2026 examination priorities earlier this week — notably omitting any mention of crypto or digital assets, another sign of the agency’s shift away from the crypto-heavy focus of the Gensler period.Despite the decline in enforcement activity, Atkins said recently that the SEC under his leadership will not be “lax”, particularly in cases involving fraud or market manipulation — including in digital asset markets, should Congress grant additional authority.Shutdown Slowed Enforcement and OversightThe 43-day government shutdown had a clear effect on the SEC’s operations:Enforcement teams were unable to pursue investigations at full capacityStaff shortages delayed routine oversightProgress on pending crypto and non-crypto cases slowedReviews of ETF applications and IPO filings were pushed backFollowing the restoration of normal operations last week, the SEC has resumed processing IPO applications, ETF reviews, and a backlog of rulemaking tasks.Congress Eyes Digital Asset Market Structure ReformMeanwhile, lawmakers are preparing for significant shifts in U.S. crypto regulation.Republican members of the Senate Banking Committee said this week they expect to pass a comprehensive digital-asset market-structure bill by early 2026.The legislation had originally been expected before year-end, but progress was derailed by:The government shutdownPushback from Senate Democrats over DeFi-related provisionsIf enacted, the bill could dramatically reshape U.S. crypto oversight, granting the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) expanded authority over digital assets and clarifying the SEC’s jurisdiction.Atkins has said the SEC will enforce securities laws firmly but consistently if the agency is given clearer authority over digital-asset markets.OutlookThe SEC’s enforcement decline reflects:Leadership transitionShifting prioritiesOperational limits during the shutdownPending legislative changes in CongressBut the respite for crypto firms may be temporary.If Congress passes a sweeping market-structure bill in early 2026, both the SEC and CFTC may soon be navigating a new era of coordinated digital-asset regulation — one where enforcement actions could rise again under revised statutory mandates.

data.title: Crypto News Today: 21Shares Expands in Europe With Six New Crypto ETPs as Global ETF Competition Heats Up
data.description: Crypto ETP giant 21Shares has expanded its European footprint with the launch of six additional exchange-traded products on Nasdaq Stockholm, adding new single-asset offerings for Aave, Cardano, Chainlink and Polkadot, as well as two multi-asset crypto baskets.The move comes as the U.S. market sees a surge of new digital-asset ETFs, including a wave of recently approved spot XRP funds.What to Know21Shares has cross-listed six new ETPs on Nasdaq Stockholm, bringing its total there to 16.The new listings include AAVE, ADA, LINK, DOT and two diversified baskets.21Shares now manages $8 billion AUM, roughly 4% of the global $191.5B crypto ETF/ETP market.Ethereum is trading around $2,823, and Bitcoin around $87,108, reflecting subdued sentiment despite ETF growth.U.S. markets are experiencing a flood of new crypto ETFs, including spot XRP ETFs launching this week.21Shares Boosts Nordic Presence With Six New ETP ListingsThe Swiss-based issuer said Thursday that it had cross-listed six new crypto ETPs on Nasdaq Stockholm, expanding access for Sweden’s growing digital-asset investor base.The new products include:Aave (AAVE) ETPCardano (ADA) ETPChainlink (LINK) ETPPolkadot (DOT) ETPTwo diversified crypto baskets targeting multi-asset exposureThe expansion brings the firm’s Swedish lineup to 16 total products, still far fewer than the dozens listed across other European exchanges such as SIX Swiss Exchange, Xetra, and Euronext Amsterdam.“We continue to see strong demand from Nordic investors seeking diversified, cost-efficient access to digital assets through regulated exchanges,”said Alistair Byas Perry, head of EU investments at 21Shares.AUM Nears $8B as 21Shares Strengthens Global LeadWith listings across the U.S. and Europe, 21Shares is now one of the largest global crypto ETP issuers, managing nearly $8 billion in assets.According to CoinShares, about half of the firm’s AUM sits in U.S. products co-issued with Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest.This accounts for roughly 4% of the global $191.5B crypto ETF/ETP ecosystem.U.S. ETF Market Heats Up: XRP Becomes Sixth Crypto With a Spot ETFThe European expansion comes during an ETF boom in the U.S., highlighted by last week’s debut of spot XRP ETFs.Canary Capital launched the first pure-play spot XRP ETF.Bitwise and Grayscale are set to follow this week and early next.XRP is now the sixth crypto to support a U.S. single-asset ETF, following:BitcoinEtherSolanaLitecoinHederaXRPETF analyst Nate Geraci said the rapid expansion underscores growing issuer competition for crypto-focused inflows.Bitcoin ETFs Struggle Despite New LaunchesDespite rising enthusiasm around new altcoin ETFs, U.S. Bitcoin ETFs continue to face significant selling pressure.On Tuesday, BlackRock’s IBIT posted a record $520M outflow, its worst since launch.Bitcoin ETF flows have fallen to $27.4B YTD, about 30% below last year’s $41.7B pace.Bitcoin itself trades near $87,108, far below its early-October peak.Ethereum trades around $2,823, down sharply from this summer’s highs.Analysts say the divergence reflects sector rotation toward new altcoin products combined with macro-driven derisking.More ETPs Coming, Competition IntensifyingETF specialists expect dozens more crypto ETFs and ETPs to launch across Europe and the U.S. in 2026, including:More single-asset fundsMore staked-asset ETF structuresMulti-asset crypto indexes targeting institutional demand21Shares’ latest expansion suggests European issuers are racing to keep pace with U.S. developments, even as market volatility keeps digital-asset prices under pressure.

data.title: India's Economic Survey May Address Stablecoin Regulations Amid RBI's Cautious Stance
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Indian government is considering the inclusion of stablecoin regulations in its Economic Survey for 2025-2026, while the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) maintains a cautious approach towards cryptocurrencies and advocates for a central bank digital currency (CBDC). This reflects a divergence in policy recommendations between the government and the central bank. The Ministry of Finance is expected to present its case for stablecoins in the annual report, which outlines key policy recommendations and the state of the economy. This information was reported by MoneyControl, citing an official familiar with the matter.

RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra emphasized the central bank's cautious stance on stablecoins during a speech at the Delhi School of Economics. He stated, "We have a very cautious approach towards crypto because of various concerns that we have. Of course, the government has to take a final view. There is a working group which was set up earlier, and they will make a final call as to how, if at all, crypto is to be handled in our country." Malhotra dismissed concerns that India needs to respond to stablecoin innovation led by the United States, following the passage of the GENIUS bill in June, citing India's robust domestic digital payments infrastructure, which includes the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), National Electronic Funds Transfer (NEFT), and Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) system.

The potential regulation of cryptocurrencies by the Indian government would mark a significant shift from its previous anti-crypto stance, potentially legitimizing digital assets in the world's most populous country. This could spur crypto adoption and potentially increase asset prices. However, officials continue to express skepticism about "unbacked" cryptocurrencies. In October, Piyush Goyal, India's minister of commerce and industry, stated that the government neither encourages nor discourages cryptocurrencies, but he also questioned the value of crypto as an asset class, noting that most cryptocurrencies lack sovereign backing or underlying assets that provide them with value.

data.title: Bitcoin Price (BTC) News: Bitcoin Falls to $87K, Losing Its Yearly Gains as Macro Volatility Hits Crypto Markets
data.description: Bitcoin has erased all of its 2025 gains and fallen sharply below its yearly open as global macro volatility intensifies, dropping to $87,289 on Thursday — its lowest level since April. Analysts say crypto markets are no longer acting as a hedge against uncertainty and are increasingly moving in lockstep with broader risk-asset sentiment.What to KnowBitcoin has fallen to $87,289, losing all of its 2025 gains.BTC is now down ~7% year-to-date and over 17% in the past month.Analysts say crypto no longer acts as a hedge but tracks traditional markets.Ethereum has slipped to $3,030, down from $3,345 at the start of the year.Glassnode says BTC may be approaching a local bottom near $94K–$100K, but price is currently below that range.Macro Shock Slams Crypto as Bitcoin Breaks Below Yearly OpenBitcoin’s slide below $90,000 this week has pulled the world’s largest cryptocurrency beneath its starting level for 2025, as renewed U.S. tariffs, persistent inflation fears and diminishing hopes for further Fed rate cuts spill across global markets.“Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies are no longer a hedge against market risk,” said Bill Maurer, director of the Institute for Money, Technology and Financial Inclusion at UC Irvine.“They now track the rest of the market because uncertainty is elevated everywhere — trade wars, inflation, geopolitical shocks, jobs worries, the Fed's stance.”Investors have been de-risking across asset classes, he added, pushing crypto into deeper volatility.Analysts: Bitcoin Behaving Like a High-Beta Risk AssetDuke University finance professor Campbell Harvey agrees:“In a risk-off situation, people dump their risky assets like stocks and bitcoin. That’s what is happening today.”He added that investor focus is shifting toward stablecoins, which he calls the sector’s “first killer app.”Bitcoin’s 2025 Rally Fully UnwoundBitcoin entered 2025 at $93,540. Since then:2025 Timeline (Updated)January: BTC begins the year at $93,540.April: Falls to $74,500 amid tariff uncertainty.October: Hits an all-time high of $126,200 (+35% YTD).November: Drops below $90,000, erasing all gains.Today: Trades at $87,289, down ~7% YTD.Ethereum has mirrored the downturn, sliding to $3,030, below its $3,345 New Year level.Glassnode: BTC Entering a Consolidation Phase — But Price Has Slipped Below the Expected Bottom ZoneBlockchain analytics platform Glassnode reported this week that oversold conditions and slowing outflows suggest Bitcoin may be forming a local bottom in the $94K–$100K range.However, with current price at $87,289, Bitcoin has broken below the lower edge of that expected support area, signaling deeper macro-driven stress than earlier anticipated.‘Fasten Your Seatbelt’: Bitcoin Volatility Is Nothing NewDespite the sharp drawdown, Harvey says history suggests the long-term picture is unchanged:“There have been six episodes where Bitcoin dropped more than 60%.Every time, it recovered and continued to appreciate.So fasten your seatbelt.”He warned, however, that Corporate Digital Asset Treasury strategies — where companies borrow or reallocate reserves into BTC — may be amplifying negative sentiment:“Why gamble a firm’s cash reserves on an asset four times as risky as the stock market?”What’s Next for Bitcoin?Analysts point to several key factors that will determine Bitcoin’s next move:Key Levels$85K–$88K is now the nearest support zone.A recovery back above $94K–$100K would signal stabilization.Macro ForcesFed policy direction at December’s meetingGlobal response to U.S. tariff and trade tensionsVolatility in equities, yields and the U.S. dollarCrypto-Specific DriversOngoing ETF outflowsLiquidation clusters in derivatives marketsWeakening liquidity across altcoinsFor now, Bitcoin remains tightly correlated with traditional markets — meaning its next major move may depend less on crypto-native developments and more on global risk sentiment.

data.title: A hardcore short-selling whale's BTC short position is now showing a floating profit of over $28.7 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, the "die-hard short" whale (0x5D2...9bb7) who shorted BTC four times in a row recently opened a 20x leveraged Bitcoin short position and now has a floating profit of over $28.7 million. Currently, its short position is about 1,232 BTC. In addition, just two days ago, the whale also placed four limit buy orders in the $75,819-$79,919 range, totaling 1,450 BTC.

data.title: XRP News: What’s Next for XRP as Bitcoin Slips Below $90K Again? Market Weakness, ETF Outflows Pressure Altcoins
data.description: XRP slipped below its key $2.10 support level on Wednesday as crypto markets came under renewed pressure from Bitcoin’s return below $90,000, weakening liquidity, and accelerating ETF outflows. Analysts say institutional activity has tapered off sharply, leaving the token vulnerable to a deeper correction.What to KnowXRP fell under the $2.10 support, signaling heightened downside risk.A sharp rebound from $2.03 saw a 28% surge in volume, but upside faded quickly.Bitcoin’s death cross, ETF outflows, and weak structure continue to drag on altcoins.Institutional traders stepped aside in late-session trading as liquidity collapsed.XRP Breaks Below Critical Support as Traders Exit PositionsXRP pierced the widely watched $2.10 floor during heavy late-session selling Wednesday. The breakdown came as traders reduced exposure ahead of a potential continuation move, mirroring weakness across the broader crypto market.The token spent most of the session trading in a volatile $2.03–$2.15 range, briefly showing resilience despite Bitcoin’s renewed breakdown. But that strength faded as the market turned sharply risk-off.News BackgroundXRP traded within a tight, volatile band amid broader crypto weakness.A rebound from $2.03 was fueled by a 177.9M volume spike, but the rally failed repeatedly at $2.14–$2.15.Market sentiment deteriorated as Bitcoin’s death cross and heavy ETF outflows weighed on risk appetite.Institutional activity dried up in the final hour of trading as XRP cracked below support.Price Action SummaryXRP slipped 1.0% over the past 24 hours, trading from $2.13 to $2.11 while navigating a choppy intraday structure.The most notable move occurred at 21:00 UTC, when a 177.9M (28% above average) volume spike propelled a swift rebound from $2.03. But each rally attempt was capped at $2.14–$2.15, forming a series of lower highs as sellers absorbed buy pressure.The session ended with a decisive breakdown from $2.124 → $2.103, cutting through the $2.10 support on elevated sell volume. Liquidity collapsed afterward, suggesting institutional traders stepped aside ahead of potential volatility.Technical Analysis: XRP Structure Turns BearishSupport & Resistance$2.10 now flips from support to immediate resistance.The market is anchored around the cycle low at $2.03.Failure to reclaim $2.14–$2.15 leaves the near-term ceiling firmly intact.Volume BehaviorThe large rebound volume confirmed strong participation.But lack of follow-through on recovery attempts signaled exhaustion.Final-hour breakdown volume triggered algorithmic momentum selling.Trend StructureXRP now prints lower highs and lower lows, consistent with early-stage continuation patterns that often precede retests of key swing supports. The broader downtrend began after repeated failures above $2.48 earlier this month.Momentum ConditionsShort-term oscillators approach oversold territory, implying potential stabilization if $2.03 holds.But without a $2.15 reclaim, any bounce risks becoming reactive, not structural.What Traders Should Watch NextXRP sits at a critical inflection zone:Key Levels$2.03 must hold to prevent a slide toward $1.91–$1.73.A breakup above $2.15 is needed to neutralize the bearish setup.Liquidity & Institutional FlowsInstitutional liquidity collapsed after the $2.10 breakdown — renewed participation will dictate direction.Watch for derivatives liquidation clusters; XRP saw ~$28M in liquidations in recent sessions.Macro & Market StructureBitcoin’s death cross, weak structure, and ongoing ETF outflows continue to weigh heavily on altcoins.A stronger Bitcoin base may be required for any meaningful XRP recovery.
